hero.title: Access to rare disease therapies when local availability is limited
hero.subtitle: We coordinate cross-border supply of treatments in complex cases.

alexandra.name: Alexandra Kireeva
alexandra.description: Over 10 years coordinating complex logistics and unregistered supplies of medicines for rare diseases.

irina.name: Irina Korotkova
irina.description: Handles therapy requests and procurement, organizes international deliveries.

sofia.name: Sofia Fleishman
sofia.description: Coordinates procurement and supply of therapies for international cases.

founder.story: The company grew out of personal experience living with a rare disease and understanding how difficult the path to therapy can be. We are building a system where that path becomes clear and manageable.
founder.quote: When access to therapy turns into a maze, it helps to have someone who knows the way.
founder.attribution: Alexandra Kireeva, founder of Aurora Med

about.intro: Aurora Med connects funds, organizations, and European suppliers so patients with rare diseases can access therapy when local availability is limited.
about.storyBody: We grew from personal experience and years of work with unregistered supplies and complex logistics. Our goal is to make the path to therapy transparent—from request to delivery.
about.missionBody: Reduce barriers between patients and lawful sources of therapy in Europe while maintaining compliance and supply-chain safety.
about.value1Body: Clear stages, realistic timelines, and accountability at every step.
about.value2Body: A single point of contact for funds, clinics, and suppliers.
about.value3Body: Vetted partners and documentation that meets regulatory expectations.

alexandra.longDescription: Alexandra has spent more than ten years on logistics for complex cases with unregistered supplies, building transparent processes for funds and patients. As founder of Aurora Med she sets the standards for coordination with European suppliers.

irina.longDescription: Irina leads communication with funds and organizations—from the first request through procurement alignment and international delivery. She turns complex pathways into clear steps and timelines.

sofia.longDescription: Sofia oversees procurement and delivery of therapies in international programs, supplier terms, and documentation quality.

order.intro: Fill out the form—we will contact you, clarify therapy and logistics details, and outline next steps.
order.success: Your request was sent. We will contact you shortly.
order.error: Could not submit the form. Please try again later or email us.

suppliers.intro: We work with European manufacturers and distributors to support deliveries in complex, non-standard cases. Tell us about your company and how you would like to collaborate.
suppliers.partnership1: We coordinate supply of orphan and rare therapies for funds and organizations, and we look for European manufacturers and distributors willing to work on individual requests, approved programs, and within applicable import rules.
suppliers.partnership2: No registration is required: leave your company details and a message—we will contact you to discuss partnership models, documentation, compliance, and logistics.
suppliers.error: Send failed. Please try again later.

contacts.intro: Reach us by email or phone—or leave a message using the form.
contacts.name: Name

faq.intro: Answers for funds, suppliers, and general topics.

q1.question: Which medicines can be ordered?
q1.answer: We coordinate supply of EU-registered medicines for individual requests and approved pathways. The exact list depends on diagnosis, registration, and supplier availability.

q2.question: How long does delivery take?
q2.answer: Timelines depend on the product, country, and paperwork. After we clarify details, we provide an indicative timeline and milestones.

q3.question: Is a license required to order?
q3.answer: Requirements depend on jurisdiction and your organization’s role. We can outline which documents are typically needed at your stage.

q1.question: How do we become a supplier?
q1.answer: Use the “For suppliers” form—we will contact you to discuss terms and compliance.

q2.question: Which supply categories do you consider?
q2.answer: We focus on orphan and rare therapies, deliveries under individual requests and approved programs.

q1.question: Which languages do you support?
q1.answer: The site and communications are available in Russian and English; we can involve partners for other languages when needed.

q2.question: Do you process personal data?
q2.answer: We process data only as needed to respond and coordinate, in line with applicable regulations.

q3.question: Where is the company registered?
q3.answer: Corporate details and jurisdiction are provided in contractual documentation and on first contact.
